message:
test(fcp-host): gateway→connector instance-binding handoff E2E (br-jkcka.5)

Adds an end-to-end regression test for the two-phase verification
model the jkcka epic makes visible in types:

1. Gateway calls `verify_unbound` (no connector InstanceId known) →
   `CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified>`
2. Token crosses the gateway→connector boundary (modeled as owning
   the token across the test steps)
3. Connector runtime calls `promote_with_instance(&connector_id)` →
   `CapabilityToken<BoundVerified>`
4. An `execute_bound_op(_: CapabilityToken<BoundVerified>)` executor
   function accepts the promoted token. Type-level enforcement.

3 tests green:
- gateway_to_connector_handoff_end_to_end: happy path all four phases
- gateway_handoff_rejects_wrong_connector_instance: promote with
  wrong instance yields error
- direct_bound_verify_matches_unbound_plus_promote: property test —
  `CapabilityVerifier::new + verify_bound` and
  `without_instance_binding + verify_unbound + promote` produce
  tokens with equivalent verified claims

The new `execute_bound_op` demonstrator signature models what real
executors (sandbox spawners, operation invokers) will look like after
jkcka.4/jkcka.8 completes the workspace migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
